Title: Comparative genomics reveals the diversification of triterpenoid biosynthesis and origin of ocotillol-type triterpenes in Panax

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: group I beta-amyrin synthase and other multifunctional triterpene synthase branch
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Oxidosqualene cyclase; squalene-hopene cyclase N-terminal domain (PF13249) and C-terminal domain (PF13243)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: alpha-amyrin; beta-amyrin; psi-taraxasterol; 3-epicabraleadiol; trace dammarenediol-II; trace ocotillol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene into multiple triterpene skeletons, mainly alpha-amyrin, beta-amyrin, psi-taraxasterol, and 3-epicabraleadiol, with minor dammarane/ocotillol products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces minor ocotillol and dammarenediol-related products, supporting an ancestral multifunctional state preceding DDS specialization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in GIL77 yeast; GC-MS; NMR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvOSC6 is a multifunctional OSC in the group I bAS/mTTS clade. 2. It mainly produces alpha-amyrin, beta-amyrin, psi-taraxasterol, and 3-epicabraleadiol, with trace dammarenediol-II and ocotillol. 3. Its product profile supports that Panax DDS evolved from a duplicated multifunctional OSC after Pg-beta WGD.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: medicinal specialized metabolites characteristic of Panax
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heterologous GAL1 induction in yeast for functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct synteny with PvOSC7, but both are on highly syntenic regions produced by the Pg-beta WGD.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome sequencing and annotation of Panax genomes at CNGBdb CNP0002878 and CNP0003588
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Multifunctionality disperses flux among multiple triterpene products rather than strongly channeling to ocotillol.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: group I beta-amyrin synthase and other multifunctional triterpene synthase branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: alpha-amyrin; beta-amyrin; psi-taraxasterol; 3-epicabraleadiol; trace dammarenediol-II; trace ocotillol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene into multiple triterpenes with a mainly amyrin-rich profile plus minor dammarane/ocotillol products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Demonstrates that a Panax group I multifunctional OSC can also form minor ocotillol-type products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in GIL77 yeast; GC-MS; NMR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PgOSC9 is a multifunctional OSC closely resembling ancestral group I bAS/mTTS enzymes. 2. It forms mainly amyrin-type products but also minor dammarenediol-II and ocotillol-type products. 3. These data support evolutionary continuity between ancestral multifunctional OSCs and Panax DDS enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: medicinal specialized metabolites characteristic of Panax
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heterologous GAL1 induction in yeast for functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as comparative evidence for DDS origin rather than as the main causal gene for natural ocotillol accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad product promiscuity may reduce yield of any single desired triterpene.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: group I beta-amyrin synthase and other multifunctional triterpene synthase branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: alpha-amyrin; beta-amyrin; psi-taraxasterol; 3-epicabraleadiol; trace dammarenediol-II; trace ocotillol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene into mainly amyrin-type triterpenes and small amounts of dammarane/ocotillol products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports the ancestral multifunctional activity from which Panax DDS likely specialized
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in GIL77 yeast; GC-MS; NMR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PnOSC5 belongs to the group I bAS/mTTS clade. 2. It yields a broad triterpene spectrum with minor dammarenediol-II and ocotillol formation. 3. Genomic context indicates relation to DDS evolution after Pg-beta WGD.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: medicinal specialized metabolites characteristic of Panax
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heterologous GAL1 induction in yeast for functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PnOSC5 is syntenically related to the tandemly duplicated PnOSC6/PnOSC7 DDS genes via Pg-beta-derived regions.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: updated P. notoginseng genome and annotation at CNGBdb CNP0003588
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuous product profile may dilute flux to desired dammarane products.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: group I beta-amyrin synthase and other multifunctional triterpene synthase branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: alpha-amyrin; beta-amyrin; psi-taraxasterol; 3-epicabraleadiol; trace dammarenediol-II; trace ocotillol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene into diverse pentacyclic triterpenes and trace dammarane/ocotillol compounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides outgroup-like evidence that non-Panax homologs retain multifunctional ancestral activity with weak dammarane/ocotillol formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in GIL77 yeast; GC-MS; NMR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CaOSC5 is a multifunctional OSC similar to the inferred ancestral state. 2. It produces mainly amyrin-related triterpenes and weak dammarane/ocotillol products. 3. Comparison with Panax DDS supports specialization after duplication in Panax.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heterologous GAL1 induction in yeast for functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously reported as a multifunctional OSC; this study additionally detected weak dammarane-type products.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: group I beta-amyrin synthase and other multifunctional triterpene synthase branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: psi-taraxasterol-enriched profile with reduced alpha-amyrin; also other multifunctional triterpene products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene into a multifunctional triterpene spectrum distinct from CaOSC5, enriched in psi-taraxasterol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Serves as comparative evidence for diversification among multifunctional OSCs related to DDS ancestors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in GIL77 yeast; GC-MS; NMR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CaOSC6 shows a product profile slightly different from CaOSC5, with more psi-taraxasterol and less alpha-amyrin. 2. It illustrates diversification among related multifunctional OSCs. 3. This supports the idea that small catalytic shifts can precede neofunctionalization and specialization.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heterologous GAL1 induction in yeast for functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included mainly for comparative functional diversity among OSC homologs.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to dammarane-type products including dammarenediol-II and unexpectedly ocotillol and 3-epicabraleadiol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ocotillol-type triterpene formation and shows that Panax DDS enzymes are multifunctional rather than dammarenediol-II-only synthases
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in GIL77 yeast; GC-MS; NMR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PgOSC11, a Panax DDS, produces ocotillol and 3-epicabraleadiol in addition to dammarenediol-II. 2. PgOSC11 mainly produces ocotillol, unlike other tested DDSs that mainly produce dammarenediol-II. 3. This reveals a direct enzymatic origin for ocotillol-type triterpenes in Panax and supports DDS specialization from a duplicated multifunctional OSC.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heterologous GAL1 induction in yeast for functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This multifunctional property of Panax DDS had not been reported previously; strongest direct evidence for ocotillol-type triterpene origin.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: comparative genomics and functional assay across Panax OSCs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shift toward ocotillol may alter dammarenediol-II output and broader ginsenoside precursor balance.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to dammarenediol-II together with ocotillol and 3-epicabraleadiol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shows that DDS multifunctionality and ocotillol-forming capability are conserved across Panax species
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in GIL77 yeast; GC-MS; NMR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PqDDS produces ocotillol and 3-epicabraleadiol in addition to dammarenediol-II. 2. Its activity indicates that ocotillol-forming DDS multifunctionality is conserved across Panax. 3. These data support a Panax-wide evolutionary origin of ocotillol-type triterpene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: bioactive medicinal triterpenes of American ginseng
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heterologous GAL1 induction in yeast for functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: P. quinquefolius lacked a full reference assembly in this study, so one DDS sequence was used.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: functional assay; one DDS sequence included without reference assembly

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene into dammarenediol-II and also ocotillol and 3-epicabraleadiol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly contributes to ocotillol-type triterpene biosynthesis in Panax by producing ocotillol as a catalytic product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in GIL77 yeast; GC-MS; NMR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvOSC7 is a DDS-clade OSC from P. vietnamensis var. fuscidiscus. 2. It produces dammarenediol-II together with ocotillol and 3-epicabraleadiol. 3. Its function supports the hypothesis that Panax ocotillol-type triterpenes can originate directly from DDS catalysis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heterologous GAL1 induction in yeast for functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located in a Pg-beta-derived syntenic region corresponding to PvOSC6, consistent with WGD-derived specialization.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: P. vietnamensis var. fuscidiscus genome at CNGBdb CNP0002878
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Specialization may reduce broader multifunctional triterpene output.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to dammarenediol-II plus ocotillol and 3-epicabraleadiol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides direct enzymatic evidence that P. notoginseng DDS contributes to ocotillol-type triterpene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in GIL77 yeast; GC-MS; NMR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PnOSC6 is a DDS-clade enzyme that produces dammarenediol-II together with ocotillol and 3-epicabraleadiol. 2. This demonstrates that DDS multifunctionality is shared in P. notoginseng. 3. Genomic evidence indicates DDS specialization after Pg-beta WGD.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: heterologous GAL1 induction in yeast for functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PnOSC6 is one of tandemly duplicated DDS genes in P. notoginseng.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: updated P. notoginseng genome at CNGBdb CNP0003588
